channel.title: Azure News Feed
channel.description: Aggregated daily news from Azure blogs

item.title: Building resilient networks for AI supercomputers
item.description: By Valerie Cutts and Jithin Jose Last fall we introduced Fairwater, the world’s most powerful AI datacenter. Delivering a system of this scale required rethinking how Azure designs supercomputers, especially the scale-out network. Today, we are sharing more about the networking innovations that...
item.dc:creator: jithinjose

item.title: Whatsapp Desktop app stuck on logo screen after new insider update
item.description: I recently installed the latest insider update OS build - (26220.8340). since then my whatsapp desktop app has stopped working. I have tried everything from removing and installing webview2, clearing cache, microsoft store reset and every other thing but nothing worked. do i need to remove this...
item.dc:creator: Daniel_Odozi

item.title: Granular Restore for Microsoft 365 Backup Reaches General Availability
item.description: Eighteen months after the product became generally available, Microsoft 365 Backup has delivered granular restore for SharePoint Online and OneDrive sites. It’s the kind of feature that most tenant administrators might have assumed is already in the program, but at least it now is. The next step is...
item.dc:creator: TonyRedmond

item.title: How do I enable Secure Boot on an MSI motherboard running Windows 11 with TPM 2.0 already enabled?
item.description: TPM 2.0 is already enabled on my MSI motherboard and there are no error messages, but I still need to enable Secure Boot to meet Windows 11 requirements. How exactly do I enable Secure Boot on an MSI motherboard?
item.dc:creator: Thatcherw

item.title: Are fonts in Windows 11 blurrier than in Windows 10?
item.description: On a 27-inch 2K monitor, after reinstalling Windows 11 Pro 25H2 from Windows 10 Pro 22H2, the fonts on the desktop, in folders, and in applications like Steam appear noticeably blurrier than in Windows 10, and they seem thinner. I’m using an AMD graphics card, and I reinstalled Windows 11 to use...
item.dc:creator: ArkansasQuapaw

item.title: The Windows 11 25H2 update keeps failing, with error codes 0x80070422 and 0x800f0922
item.description: I received the Windows 11 25H2 update notification two months ago, but after trying more than a dozen times, I still can’t install it. The system keeps returning error codes 0x80070422 and 0x800f0922. What could be causing this, and how can I fix it?
item.dc:creator: Castiellm

item.title: Turning GitHub Copilot into a “Best Practices Coach” with Copilot Spaces + a Markdown Knowledge Base
item.description: Why Copilot Spaces + Markdown repos work so well When you ask Copilot generic questions (“How should we log errors?” “What’s our API versioning approach?”), the model will often respond with reasonable defaults. But reasonable defaults are not the same as your standards. Copilot Spaces solve the...
item.dc:creator: mohashaikh

item.title: How to clear up or delete disk space on mac
item.description: Hi everyone,I'm a bit of a Mac newbie and I’ve run into a wall—my Mac is telling me that my disk is almost full. I'm starting to see performance issues, but I'm terrified of deleting something important that the system needs to run. How can I delete disk space on mac?My Setup:Mac Model: MacBook Pro...
item.dc:creator: Brondam

item.title: 🚀 Modernizing Azure Landing Zone Deployments: From Terraform Scripts to GHCP-Driven Engineering
item.description: The code nobody wants to write anymore Most Azure Landing Zone (ALZ) deployments we see today still follow the same pattern they did a few years ago. Engineers manually write Terraform modules, copy networking blocks from older repos, tweak management group hierarchies, and wire pipelines step by...
item.dc:creator: gurkirat

item.title: Feature Request: Custom Status Labels for Calendar Events (e.g., 'In a Training')
item.description: Hey Teams Community! 👋 I have a small but impactful feature idea and would love your votes and feedback. --- 🔴 The Problem Right now, every calendar block in Microsoft Teams shows the same status to colleagues: "In a Meeting." Whether you're in a 10-minute standup or a 3-hour mandatory training —...
item.dc:creator: tarun1992

item.title: Operationalizing Responsible AI in Microsoft Foundry within Enterprise Network Boundaries
item.description: Strategic Overview Deploying Microsoft Foundry within a VNet-integrated landing zone requires a thoughtful balance between innovation and enterprise-grade security, especially in highly regulated industries like banking. This architecture enforces Responsible AI (RAI) principles and robust content...
item.dc:creator: Shruti9162

item.title: Running engagement surveys through M365 without buying another tool?
item.description: HR asked me if we can run quarterly employee engagement surveys using our existing M365 stack. We have E3 licenses. I tried setting up something with Forms but the analytics are super basic and theres no way to track trends over time or slice by department without a bunch of manual Excel work....
item.dc:creator: ElisaDelaney

item.title: Best way to enforce manager check-ins across the org using M365 tools?
item.description: Our CHRO wants every manager to have at least bi-weekly check-ins with directs and wants visibility into whether its actually happening. Right now theres zero tracking. Some managers do it, some dont, and leadership has no idea. I looked at Viva Insights but it only shows meeting frequency, not...
item.dc:creator: JordanT86

item.title: Use Azure Container Registry as an Upstream Source for Artifact Cache
item.description: In collaboration with:Luis Dieguez, Principal Software Engineering Manager, Azure Container RegistryAkash Singhal, Senior Software Engineer, Azure Container RegistryKiran Challa, Senior Software Engineer, Azure Container RegistryNathan Anderson, Senior Software Engineer, Azure Container...
item.dc:creator: toddysm

item.title: Public Preview: Migrate Availability Sets to Virtual Machine Scale Sets
item.description: We're excited to announce the public preview of availability set to Virtual Machine Scale Set migration for Azure Virtual Machines. This new capability enables you to move your existing VMs from availability sets to Virtual Machine Scale Sets with Flexible orchestration—unlocking higher...
item.dc:creator: micahmckittrick

item.title: Secret scanning with GitHub MCP Server is now generally available
item.description: GitHub secret scanning in the GitHub MCP (Model Context Protocol) server is now generally available. When you use an MCP-compatible AI coding agent or IDE (like GitHub Copilot CLI or… The post Secret scanning with GitHub MCP Server is now generally available appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Better together with Azure WAF + Microsoft Defender for Storage + Defender for Azure SQL Databases
item.description: Authored by: Fernanda_Vela​ , saikishor​, Yura_Lee​ Reviewed by: YuriDiogenes​, Mohit_Kumar​, Amir_Dahan​, eitanbremler​ , Kitt_Weatherman​ Introduction Often, customers ask why additional workload protection is needed when a web application firewall is already in place. Azure Web Application...
item.dc:creator: Yura_Lee

item.title: Dependency scanning with GitHub MCP Server is in public preview
item.description: The GitHub MCP Server can now scan your code changes for vulnerable dependencies before you commit or open a pull request. You’ll catch known vulnerabilities while you write code with… The post Dependency scanning with GitHub MCP Server is in public preview appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Update host keys to use SFTP on Azure Blob Storage
item.description: Update host keys to use SFTP on Azure Blob Storage Some Azure Storage users may receive periodically an alert informing to update host keys to use SFTP on Azure Blob Storage. This article describes the impact this will have on the SFTP blob service and what are the changes needed.Table of Content...
item.dc:creator: LuisFilipe

item.title: windows updating loop
item.description: Hello, I'm facing a loop of windows updating and then showing " making changes" for a couple of weeks can anybody tell me how to solve this problem ??
item.dc:creator: Kishalay104

item.title: Extending Sentinel Data Integration: Azure Blob Storage Support for CCF Connectors
item.description: As organizations scale their security operations, the ability to ingest, process, and analyze high volumes of data reliably becomes increasingly critical. Microsoft Sentinel continues to expand its ecosystem through the Codeless Connector Framework (CCF), enabling ISVs to build and deliver...
item.dc:creator: JesseKopavi

item.title: Welcome to Maintainer Month: Celebrating the people behind the code
item.description: What maintainers are telling us, what we've shipped, and how to celebrate the people behind open source. The post Welcome to Maintainer Month: Celebrating the people behind the code appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Abigail Cabunoc Mayes

item.title: Code-to-cloud risk visibility with Microsoft Defender for Cloud is now generally available
item.description: This integration is now generally available. Since entering public preview, we’ve heard valuable feedback from customers, and we’ve shipped follow-up improvements that bring artifact and runtime context closer to the… The post Code-to-cloud risk visibility with Microsoft Defender for Cloud is now...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Windows 12 — Almost Ready
item.description: After a thorough analysis of build 29580, I’ve identified several indicators pointing toward the next-generation Windows experience:- the system contains new wallpapers prepared for the upcoming release - there are new UI icons and refreshed interface elements present in the build - the build...
item.dc:creator: kikero_exe

item.title: Deprecation notice: code_scanning_upload field will be removed from rate_limit API endpoint
item.description: On May 19, 2026, we’ll remove the code_scanning_upload field from the rate_limit REST API endpoint response. Why did we make this change? The code_scanning_upload field in the rate_limit response has… The post Deprecation notice: code_scanning_upload field will be removed from rate_limit API...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: No More All-or-Nothing: Smarter, Granular Data Recovery in Microsoft 365 Backup
item.description: Data recovery has long carried a kind of quiet dread in the IT world. When something goes wrong whether it’s a user accidentally deleting an important file, a sync error wiping out folders, or a security incident compromising data the recovery process often feels like a blunt instrument....
item.dc:creator: JohnNaguib

item.title: From Manual Backfills to Autonomous Pipelines: Building an LLM-Powered Backfill Agent on Azure
item.description: Introduction: Data backfills are a common operational requirement in modern data platforms. Missing partitions, upstream delays, or failed pipeline runs often require engineers to manually identify gaps, determine the appropriate recovery window, and trigger reprocessing. This approach does not...
item.dc:creator: Sally_Dabbah

item.title: Automating Azure Naming Standards using API and DevOps Pipelines
item.description: Introduction In large Azure environments, one of the most overlooked yet critical governance challenges is resource naming consistency. While organizations define naming standards, enforcing them at scale across multiple subscriptions, teams, and pipelines often becomes a manual and inconsistent...
item.dc:creator: sameenamohammed

item.title: Deploying Azure Resources with Managed HSM Keys Using Bicep
item.description: Architecture Overview The deployment includes: Managed HSM instance Key creation inside HSM User-assigned managed identity / service principal Role assignments for key access Azure resource (e.g., Storage / Databricks / Disk) using CMK Flow: Create Managed HSM Create encryption key Assign...
item.dc:creator: Roslin_Nivetha

item.title: How File-Level Archiving Works for SharePoint Online
item.description: SharePoint Online supports file-level archiving, meaning that you can send individual files (most types) to Microsoft 365 Archive to be held in cold storage. Individual users can make the decision about what files need to be online instead of forcing administrators to decide whether to archive...
item.dc:creator: TonyRedmond

item.title: Moving Beyond Prompts: A Practical Introduction to Spec-Driven Development
item.description: In the last year, many of us have started writing code differently. We describe what we want, let AI generate an answer, review it, tweak the prompt, and try again. This loop—prompt, retry, adjust—has quietly become part of our daily workflow. At first, it feels incredibly productive. But as the...
item.dc:creator: sutandan

item.title: Building an AI Agent for Azure Infrastructure Validation
item.description: 1. Introduction Infrastructure consistency is critical in large-scale Azure environments, especially in migration programs and DevOps-driven deployments. While Infrastructure as Code (IaC) using Terraform improves reproducibility, it does not fully eliminate: Manual errors in design specifications...
item.dc:creator: ranjsharma

item.title: Policy applied allthough it shouldn't
item.description: Hi, all of a sudden Intune chaanges its behavior. I have a policy in place that sets persistent browser session. On the device filter tab I excluded devices with this syntax:device.trustType -eq "ServerAD" -or device.deviceOwnership -eq "Company" Starting last week I have to re-authenticate on a...
item.dc:creator: heinzelrumpel

item.title: Performance Tuning and Scaling Optimization for Large-Scale Azure Workloads
item.description: Summary As cloud-native systems scale, performance challenges rarely stem from a single bottleneck. Instead, they emerge from the interaction between compute, orchestration, and data layers under load. This article captures a practical optimization journey of a high-volume Azure-based workload and...
item.dc:creator: ruchitapradhan

item.title: Building Multi-File Refactoring Agents with GitHub Copilot Workspace
item.description: Introduction AI-assisted development continues to evolve beyond inline code suggestions toward end-to-end engineering workflows. While tools such as GitHub Copilot have significantly improved developer productivity at the function and file level, modern applications demand capabilities that operate...
item.dc:creator: Devi_Priya

item.title: Build and Deploy Logic App Workflows Using Visual Studio Code and CI/CD Pipeline
item.description: Throughout this guide, you'll create a Standard logic app workspace and project, build your workflow, and deploy it as a Standard logic app resource in Azure. This enables your workflow to run in a single-tenant Azure Logic Apps environment or within an App Service Environment v3 (restricted to...
item.dc:creator: Devi_Priya

item.title: Lock down AI, web, and private apps: what’s new in Internet Access and Private Access
item.description: One theme is crystal clear across the security industry: AI is transforming security, and security must transform with it. Organizations everywhere are embracing generative AI to boost productivity and accelerate innovation. But with this rapid adoption comes new challenges that security teams...
item.dc:creator: Sinead_ODonovan

item.title: Tableau to Power BI Migration: Semantic Layer-First Approach for Cloud Architects
item.description: Author's: Mahjabin Ahmed, Lavanya Sreedhar LavanyaSreedhar​, Peter Lo PeterLo​, Aryan Anmol aryananmol​, Shreya Harvu shreyaharvu​ and Rafia Aqil Rafia_Aqil​ In this guide, we provide practical guidance for migrating from Tableau to Power BI, with a focus on technical best practices and...
item.dc:creator: Rafia_Aqil

item.title: Customer Managed Keys for Encryption at Rest
item.description: A new set of content on Azure Citadel covering Customer Managed Keys (CMK) for encryption at rest — the first phase in a series that will also cover confidential compute. Overview I am pleased to launch the new Customer Managed Keys area on Azure Citadel. Controlling your encryption keys is one of...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Proposal: “Filename Printer” option for DOCX/PDF print jobs in Windows 11
item.description: I’d like to propose a small but high‑impact Windows 11 printing feature that would help anyone who prints multiple revisions of the same document (legal, academic, enterprise, etc.). The idea is a built‑in “Filename Printer” option that, for DOCX and PDF print jobs, can insert a single removable...
item.dc:creator: MadhavKavuru

item.title: Build a secure identity foundation: 5 webinars to strengthen access management with Microsoft Entra
item.description: To avoid gaps in protection, organizations need to fully deploy and scale core identity protections–from phishing-resistant authentication to Conditional Access policies to risk remediation and resiliency–across cloud and hybrid deployments. That’s why we’re launching the Secure identity foundation...
item.dc:creator: Melanie_Maynes

item.title: Java OpenJDK April 2026 Patch & Security Update
item.description: Hello Java customers! We are happy to announce the latest April 2026 patch & security update release for the Microsoft Build of OpenJDK. Check our release notes page for details on fixes and enhancements or download and install the binaries today. The source code of our builds are now available on...
item.dc:creator: Josh Martin-Jaffe

item.title: What’s new in Microsoft Sentinel: RSAC 2026
item.description: Security is entering a new era, one defined by explosive data growth, increasingly sophisticated threats, and the rise of AI-enabled operations. To keep pace, security teams need an AI-powered approach to collect, reason over, and act on security data at scale. At RSA Conference 2026 (RSAC), we’re...
item.dc:creator: spalani

item.title: What’s new in Microsoft Sentinel: April 2026
item.description: Welcome to the April 2026 edition of What's new in Microsoft Sentinel. April brings a broad set of updates, with RSAC 2026 announcements rolling out alongside new features. Highlights include cost limit enforcement to prevent runaway query costs, curated open-source intelligence in Threat...
item.dc:creator: vkokkengada

item.title: Architecture to Resilience: A Decision Guide
item.description: Start with the framework, accelerate with the tool Watch the video walkthrough The Application Resilience Framework originated from a practical gap we saw in resilience reviews: teams had architecture diagrams, monitoring data, incident history, and runbooks, but no consistent way to connect them...
item.dc:creator: varghesejoji

item.title: Logic Apps Newsletter - May 2026
item.description: In this issue: Ace Aviator of the Month News from our product group News from our community Ace Aviator of the Month May 2026's Ace Aviator: Yahya Ajwad Yahya Ajwad - Chief Architect Cloud Integration @ Epical What's your role and title? What are your responsibilities? I work as Chief Architect and...
item.dc:creator: WSilveira

item.title: SonarPilot - Bulk Managing SonarQube Issues
item.description: Stop Managing SonarQube Issues One by One, Introducing SonarPilot Introduction If you've worked on a large projects, you know the feeling. You open your SonarQube dashboard, and you're staring at 30,000+ open issues spread across many rules. You need to triage them before the next sprint. You need...
item.dc:creator: JatinderSingh0211

item.title: Microsoft Agent Framework – Building Blocks for AI Part 3
item.description: Build intelligent AI agents in .NET with the Microsoft Agent Framework. Learn how to create agents with tools, multi-turn conversations, memory, and graph-based workflows that bring together the building blocks from Parts 1 and 2. The post Microsoft Agent Framework – Building Blocks for AI Part 3...
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Likness

item.title: Introducing selective response actions for high-value assets in Microsoft Defender
item.description: Deploying Microsoft Defender on high-value assets (HVAs) such as domain controllers, ADFS servers, and other Tier-0 systems, requires a thoughtful approach to balance strong protection with operational stability. Given the powerful response capabilities available, organizations often seek greater...
item.dc:creator: amibarayev

item.title: Microsoft Defender for Cloud Customer Newsletter
item.description: What's new in Defender for Cloud? Container runtime anti-malware detection and blocking and DNS Detection for Kubernetes is now GA in Defender for Containers for AKS, EKS, and GKE. Learn more about these announcements here and here. Defender for Storage integration in Azure Portal Storage Center...
item.dc:creator: Yura_Lee
